intended_use: RadioLens v1.0 software is a medical diagnostic application that displays, processes, stores, and transfers DICOM data, with the exception of mammography. It provides the capability to store images an
intended_use_full: RadioLens v1.0 software is a medical diagnostic application that displays, processes, stores, and transfers DICOM data, with the exception of mammography. It provides the capability to store images and patient information, perform filtering, digital manipulation, and quantitative measurements. RadioLens v1.0 includes an optional SpindleX module which is used to analyze potential vertebral body displacements in spine, either absolute or relative, using cervical/lumbar digital X-Rays of spine. A qualified medical practitioner may use the module to semi-automatically identify relevant spine anatomy and calculate vertebral displacement measurements. Measurements are then used to determine severity and location of any spinal ligament injury leading to subluxation using criteria published in AMA Guides to The Evaluation of Permanent Impairment, 5th & 6th edition. SpindleX is meant for assistance with analysis of spinal ligament injury in the adult population (>18 years of age) as captured in stress x-rays.
target_condition: spinal ligament injury with potential subluxation in the cervical and lumbar spine
ground_truth: Morphometry points pre-marked on cervical and lumbar images by US board-certified radiologists who used these to prepare clinical reports delivered to patients
other_metrics: Body part detection sensitivity: 100% (cervical/lumbar identification). Position detection sensitivities: flexion 96.8%, extension 92.0%, neutral 90.5%. Intraclass correlation coefficients (ICC) for measurements ranged from 0.656 to 0.997, with model agreement equivalent to radiologist-to-radiologist agreement. Cervical measurements: Stress Lines ICC 0.953 (Excellent), Canal Diameter ICC 0.955 (Excellent), Vertebral Offset Neutral ICC 0.707 (Moderate), Angular MSI ICC 0.781 (Good), Translational Motion 6th Edition Flexion ICC 0.802 (Good). Lumbar measurements: Stress Lines ICC 0.968 (Excellent), Canal Diameter ICC 0.784 (Good), Ferguson's Angle ICC 0.939 (Excellent), Integrity of 3rd Lumbar Vertebra ICC 0.996 (Excellent), Vertebral Offset Neutral ICC 0.777 (Good), Angular MSI ICC 0.771 (Good), Translational Motion 6th Edition Flexion ICC 0.774 (Good), Translational Motion 6th Edition Extension ICC 0.769 (Good).
